components.title: Chart
components.summary: The SwiftUI view that hosts marks and renders a data-driven chart, generating scales, axes, and legends automatically.
components.summaryLong: Root container. Everything in this category is configured by attaching chart* view modifiers to a Chart and by composing ChartContent marks inside its builder.
components.higNotes: Choose the chart type that matches the comparison the user must make: bars for category comparison, lines for trends over time, area for cumulative magnitude, sector for part-to-whole. Keep marks under ~7 series; rely on automatic scales/legends before customizing. Always supply accessible value labels via .value(_:).

properties.name: plotHeight
properties.notes: Drives .frame(height:) on the Chart for the showroom. Range 120...500.

properties.name: showLegend
properties.notes: Maps to .chartLegend(_:). Legend appears only when marks are grouped via foregroundStyle(by:).

properties.name: legendPosition
properties.notes: Maps to .chartLegend(position:). tvOS/watchOS layout space is tight; prefer bottom.

properties.name: xAxisVisible
properties.notes: false maps to .chartXAxis(.hidden).

properties.name: yAxisVisible
properties.notes: false maps to .chartYAxis(.hidden).

properties.name: plotBackground
properties.notes: Maps to .chartPlotStyle { $0.background(...) }.

properties.name: scrollableX
properties.notes: Enables .chartScrollableAxes(.horizontal) for long content; pair with .chartXVisibleDomain.

properties.name: selectionEnabled
properties.notes: Enables .chartXSelection(value:) tap/drag selection with a scrolling RuleMark annotation.

components.codeTemplate:
Chart {
    BarMark(
        x: .value("Category", item.category),
        y: .value("Value", item.value)
    )
}
.frame(height: {plotHeight})
.chartLegend({showLegend}, position: {legendPosition})
.chartXAxis({xAxisVisible} ? .automatic : .hidden)
.chartYAxis({yAxisVisible} ? .automatic : .hidden)
.chartPlotStyle { plotArea in
    plotArea.background({plotBackground})
}
.chartScrollableAxes({scrollableX} ? .horizontal : [])
.chartXSelection(value: {selectionEnabled} ? $selectedX : .constant(nil))

components.title: BarMark
components.summary: Represents data as rectangular bars; supports vertical, horizontal, 1D, interval (Gantt), and stacked layouts.
components.higNotes: Use bars to compare discrete categories or show binned distributions (histograms). Horizontal bars scale to many categories and label easily. Stack only when the parts sum to a meaningful whole; otherwise group side-by-side with position(by:).

properties.name: orientation
properties.notes: Vertical = category on x, value on y. Horizontal swaps the axes in the initializer.

properties.name: stacking
properties.notes: normalized = 100% stacked; center = diverging; unstacked overlays. Passed as stacking: parameter.

properties.name: barWidth
properties.notes: width: .ratio(0.6), .fixed(20), or .inset(4). Controls thickness.

properties.name: cornerRadius
properties.notes: Maps to .cornerRadius(_:). Range 0...20. Rounds bar ends.

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Single-series color via .foregroundStyle(_:).

properties.name: groupByCategory
properties.notes: true adds .foregroundStyle(by:) + .position(by:) to color and offset bars per subcategory.

properties.name: opacity
properties.notes: Maps to .opacity(_:). Range 0...1.

properties.name: annotationPosition
properties.notes: Drives .annotation(position:) to label each bar with its value.

components.title: LineMark
components.summary: Connects data points with line segments to show trends; supports multiple series, interpolation, and symbols.
components.higNotes: Use lines for continuous trends over an ordered/temporal domain. Group multiple lines by series for comparison and color via foregroundStyle(by:). Add symbols only when individual points carry meaning or for accessibility differentiation.

properties.name: interpolationMethod
properties.notes: Maps to .interpolationMethod(_:). monotone/catmullRom smooth curves; step* for discrete jumps.

properties.name: lineWidth
properties.notes: Set via .lineStyle(StrokeStyle(lineWidth:)). Range 0.5...10.

properties.name: lineCap
properties.notes: StrokeStyle.lineCap.

properties.name: dashed
properties.notes: true sets StrokeStyle(dash: [6, 4]) for a dashed line.

properties.name: symbol
properties.notes: Maps to .symbol(_:). Marks each data point; aids accessibility/color-blind users.

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Single-series via .foregroundStyle(_:); use groupBySeries for multi.

properties.name: groupBySeries
properties.notes: true adds .foregroundStyle(by:) to render one colored line per series category.

components.codeTemplate:
LineMark(
    x: .value("Date", item.date),
    y: .value("Value", item.value)
)
.interpolationMethod({interpolationMethod})
.lineStyle(StrokeStyle(
    lineWidth: {lineWidth},
    lineCap: {lineCap},
    dash: {dashed} ? [6, 4] : []
))
.symbol({symbol})
.foregroundStyle({foregroundStyle})

components.title: AreaMark
components.summary: Fills the region under a line or between two bounds; supports stacked, normalized, streamgraph, and range-area charts.
components.higNotes: Use area to emphasize cumulative magnitude or part-to-whole over a continuous domain. Use normalized for 100% composition, center for streamgraphs, and a yStart/yEnd range for min-max bands. Apply gradients sparingly so data remains legible.

properties.name: stacking
properties.notes: center yields a streamgraph; normalized yields a 100% area chart.

properties.name: interpolationMethod
properties.notes: Smooths or steps the fill boundary.

properties.name: rangeMode
properties.notes: true uses the yStart/yEnd initializer to draw a min-max band instead of fill-to-baseline.

properties.name: useGradient
properties.notes: true applies a LinearGradient foregroundStyle fading to clear at the bottom.

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Base fill color.

properties.name: opacity
properties.notes: Range 0...1; areas often read better translucent.

properties.name: groupByCategory
properties.notes: true adds .foregroundStyle(by:) for stacked multi-series areas.

components.codeTemplate:
AreaMark(
    x: .value("Date", item.date),
    y: .value("Value", item.value),
    stacking: {stacking}
)
.interpolationMethod({interpolationMethod})
.foregroundStyle({useGradient} ? AnyShapeStyle(LinearGradient(colors: [{foregroundStyle}, .clear], startPoint: .top, endPoint: .bottom)) : AnyShapeStyle({foregroundStyle}))
.opacity({opacity})

components.title: PointMark
components.summary: Plots individual data points as symbols for scatter plots and bubble charts.
components.higNotes: Use points for scatter plots showing correlation between two quantitative variables. Encode a third dimension via symbolSize (bubble) or foregroundStyle(by:). Differentiate categories with both color and symbol shape for color-blind accessibility.

properties.name: symbol
properties.notes: Maps to .symbol(_:).

properties.name: symbolSize
properties.notes: Maps to .symbolSize(_:) in points². Range 10...300. Drive by a data value for bubble charts.

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Point fill color.

properties.name: opacity
properties.notes: Range 0...1; lowering opacity reveals overlapping points.

properties.name: groupByCategory
properties.notes: true adds .foregroundStyle(by:) and .symbol(by:) for categorical scatter.

components.codeTemplate:
PointMark(
    x: .value("X", item.x),
    y: .value("Y", item.y)
)
.symbol({symbol})
.symbolSize({symbolSize})
.foregroundStyle({foregroundStyle})
.opacity({opacity})

components.title: RuleMark
components.summary: Draws a horizontal or vertical reference line, threshold, or interval span across the plot.
components.higNotes: Use rules for thresholds, averages, goals, or to mark a selected x position. Style them subtly (thin, often dashed, muted color) so they support rather than dominate the primary marks. Label them with .annotation for context.

properties.name: orientation
properties.notes: horizontal uses y: (threshold); vertical uses x: (marker). Interval variants use xStart/xEnd or yStart/yEnd.

properties.name: lineWidth
properties.notes: StrokeStyle(lineWidth:). Range 0.5...6.

properties.name: dashed
properties.notes: true sets dash: [4, 4]; thresholds read best dashed.

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Reference-line color.

properties.name: annotationPosition
properties.notes: Drives .annotation(position:) for the label.

components.title: RectangleMark
components.summary: Fills rectangular cells defined by intervals on both axes; ideal for heat maps and matrix charts.
components.higNotes: Use rectangles for heat maps that encode a quantitative value as color across two categorical axes. Pair with .chartForegroundStyleScale and a continuous color gradient, and always show a legend so the color-to-value mapping is clear.

properties.name: valueColor
properties.notes: Base hue; intensity comes from foregroundStyle(by:) + chartForegroundStyleScale gradient.

properties.name: useContinuousScale
properties.notes: true encodes value as a continuous color via .foregroundStyle(by: .value(...)) with a gradient scale.

properties.name: cellWidth
properties.notes: width: controls horizontal cell size; .inset adds spacing between cells.

properties.name: cellHeight
properties.notes: height: controls vertical cell size.

properties.name: opacity
properties.notes: Range 0...1.

components.title: SectorMark
components.summary: Renders a pie or donut sector sized by angular proportion of a total; donut when innerRadius > 0.
components.higNotes: Limit to 5-7 sectors and group the remainder into 'Other'. Require positive values. A small angularInset and cornerRadius improve readability without distorting proportions. Prefer horizontal bars when precise comparison matters.

properties.name: innerRadiusRatio
properties.notes: innerRadius: .ratio(_). 0 = pie, >0 = donut. Range 0...0.9.

properties.name: outerRadiusInset
properties.notes: outerRadius: .inset(_). Range 0...40.

properties.name: angularInset
properties.notes: Gap between sectors. Range 0...8. Improves contrast/accessibility.

properties.name: cornerRadius
properties.notes: Maps to .cornerRadius(_:). Range 0...12. Keep small to avoid distortion.

properties.name: groupByCategory
properties.notes: true adds .foregroundStyle(by:) so each category gets a distinct color + legend.

components.codeTemplate:
SectorMark(
    angle: .value("Value", item.value),
    innerRadius: .ratio({innerRadiusRatio}),
    outerRadius: .inset({outerRadiusInset}),
    angularInset: {angularInset}
)
.cornerRadius({cornerRadius})
.foregroundStyle(by: .value("Category", item.name))

components.title: BarPlot (vectorized)
components.summary: Vectorized bar content that plots an entire data collection with one plot for high-performance large datasets.
components.higNotes: Use vectorized plots when rendering large collections; they are far more performant than per-element ForEach of BarMark. Apply KeyPath-based modifiers before simple-value modifiers, as the API requires.

properties.name: xKeyPath
properties.notes: x: .value("X", \.keyPath). KeyPath into the element type.

properties.name: yKeyPath
properties.notes: y: .value("Y", \.keyPath).

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Simple-value modifier; apply after KeyPath modifiers.

properties.name: groupByKeyPath
properties.notes: true uses .foregroundStyle(by: .value("Group", \.group)) for categorical coloring.

components.title: LinePlot (vectorized / function)
components.summary: Vectorized line content for whole collections and for plotting mathematical, parametric, or piecewise functions.
components.higNotes: Prefer LinePlot for large series and for analytic functions (y = f(x), parametric x/y over t). Combine multiple function plots with distinct foregroundStyle(by:) and set explicit chartXScale/chartYScale domains to frame the curve.

properties.name: mode
properties.notes: collection plots data; function plots y=f(x); parametric plots (x,y) over t with a domain.

properties.name: lineWidth
properties.notes: .lineStyle(StrokeStyle(lineWidth:)). Range 0.5...10.

properties.name: lineCap
properties.notes: StrokeStyle.lineCap.

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Curve color.

properties.name: opacity
properties.notes: Range 0...1.

components.title: AreaPlot (vectorized / function)
components.summary: Vectorized area content for whole collections and for filling under a plotted function.
components.higNotes: Use AreaPlot to shade the region under an analytic function or to render large area series efficiently. Use the yStart/yEnd function form for filled bands between two curves.

properties.name: mode
properties.notes: band uses yStart/yEnd closures to fill between two functions.

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Fill color.

properties.name: useGradient
properties.notes: true applies a LinearGradient fading to clear.

properties.name: opacity
properties.notes: Range 0...1.

components.title: PointPlot (vectorized)
components.summary: Vectorized point content for plotting large scatter collections with one performant plot.
components.higNotes: Use PointPlot for dense scatter plots over large datasets. Encode extra dimensions with symbolSize (by KeyPath) and foregroundStyle(by:) and keep opacity below 1 to reveal density.

properties.name: xKeyPath
properties.notes: x: .value("X", \.keyPath).

properties.name: yKeyPath
properties.notes: y: .value("Y", \.keyPath).

properties.name: symbolSize
properties.notes: .symbolSize(_:) in points². Range 10...200.

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Point color.

properties.name: opacity
properties.notes: Range 0...1.

components.title: SectorPlot (vectorized)
components.summary: Vectorized sector content that renders an entire pie or donut from a collection with one plot.
components.higNotes: Same composition rules as SectorMark (5-7 slices, positive values, small inset). Use the vectorized form when the slice collection is large or data-driven.

properties.name: angleKeyPath
properties.notes: angle: .value("Value", \.keyPath).

properties.name: innerRadiusRatio
properties.notes: innerRadius: .ratio(_). Range 0...0.9.

properties.name: angularInset
properties.notes: Range 0...8.

properties.name: foregroundStyleKeyPath
properties.notes: .foregroundStyle(by: .value("Category", \.keyPath)).

components.title: RulePlot & RectanglePlot (vectorized)
components.summary: Vectorized rule and rectangle content for plotting collections of reference lines or rectangular cells with one plot each.
components.higNotes: Use the vectorized RulePlot/RectanglePlot for data-driven collections of reference lines or heat-map cells where a per-element ForEach of RuleMark/RectangleMark would be expensive.

properties.name: markType
properties.notes: Switches between RulePlot and RectanglePlot template output.

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Mark color.

properties.name: lineWidth
properties.notes: Rule stroke width. Range 0.5...6.

properties.name: opacity
properties.notes: Range 0...1.

components.title: Chart3D
components.summary: A three-dimensional chart container for spatial visualizations such as surface plots and 3D scatter.
components.higNotes: New in OS 26. Use 3D charts only when the third spatial dimension genuinely aids comprehension (e.g. terrain, multivariate surfaces). Provide pose/camera controls and clear axis labels. Not available on tvOS or watchOS.

properties.name: cameraProjection
properties.notes: Maps to .chart3DCameraProjection(_:).

properties.name: pose
properties.notes: Drives .chart3DPose(_:) initial orientation.

properties.name: interactive
properties.notes: Allows drag-to-rotate of the 3D scene.

properties.name: zAxisLabel
properties.notes: 3D adds a third spatial axis. Configure z extent via .chartZScale(domain:); SurfacePlot/3D PointMark/RuleMark/RectangleMark all take an explicit z value.

components.title: Axis Customization
components.summary: Customizes axis ticks, grid lines, and value labels via chartXAxis/chartYAxis with AxisMarks builders.
components.higNotes: Keep axes clean: limit tick density, format value labels for the data type (dates, currency), and use grid lines sparingly. Hide redundant axes. Use AxisMarkValues.automatic(desiredCount:) to control tick count instead of hardcoding.

properties.name: axis
properties.notes: Selects chartXAxis vs chartYAxis to customize.

properties.name: axisPosition
properties.notes: AxisMarks(position:) location of the axis.

properties.name: tickValues
properties.notes: AxisMarks(values:). desiredCount controls tick density.

properties.name: desiredTickCount
properties.notes: Used with .automatic(desiredCount:). Range 2...12.

properties.name: showGridLines
properties.notes: Toggles AxisGridLine() in the AxisMarks builder.

properties.name: showTicks
properties.notes: Toggles AxisTick() in the builder.

properties.name: showValueLabels
properties.notes: Toggles AxisValueLabel().

properties.name: labelOrientation
properties.notes: AxisValueLabel(orientation:). Rotate labels to avoid collisions.

components.title: Scales & Domains
components.summary: Controls the mapping from data to plot via chartXScale/chartYScale domain, range, and scale type.
components.higNotes: Let scales be automatic unless a fixed domain aids comparison across charts or you must include zero. Log scales suit data spanning orders of magnitude; always label them clearly so the nonlinearity is obvious.

properties.name: axis
properties.notes: Selects chartXScale vs chartYScale.

properties.name: scaleType
properties.notes: Passed via .chartYScale(type:). Quantitative scales only for numeric data.

properties.name: useFixedDomain
properties.notes: true supplies an explicit domain: closed range instead of automatic.

properties.name: domainLowerBound
properties.notes: Lower bound when useFixedDomain is true. Range -100...100.

properties.name: domainUpperBound
properties.notes: Upper bound when useFixedDomain is true. Range 0...1000.

properties.name: zAxisAvailable
properties.notes: chartZScale(domain:range:type:) exists for Chart3D (OS 26). The spec's axis enum (x/y) omits the z option used by 3D charts.

components.title: Foreground Style Scale & Legend Grouping
components.summary: Maps categorical or continuous data to colors via foregroundStyle(by:) and customizes the color scale and legend.
components.higNotes: Use color to encode a single categorical or quantitative dimension. Provide a legend whenever color carries meaning. Choose color-blind-safe palettes and pair color with shape (symbol) where differentiation is critical.

properties.name: scaleMode
properties.notes: discrete maps categories to a palette; continuous maps a value range to a gradient.

properties.name: paletteColor1
properties.notes: First color in the discrete range / gradient start.

properties.name: paletteColor2
properties.notes: Second color.

properties.name: paletteColor3
properties.notes: Third color (discrete only).

components.title: Chart Selection & Scrolling Annotations
components.summary: Adds interactive value selection (tap/drag) on an axis, surfacing a binding used to draw a RuleMark with a scrolling annotation.
components.higNotes: Use chartXSelection for scrubbing data on touch. Reflect the selection with a RuleMark plus an .annotation that overflow-resolves within the plot bounds so it never clips. Combine with .chartScrollableAxes and .chartXVisibleDomain for paged time-series.

properties.name: selectionAxis
properties.notes: chartXSelection / chartYSelection / both.

properties.name: selectionMode
properties.notes: value binds a single Plottable; range binds a ClosedRange for brushing.

properties.name: scrollableAxes
properties.notes: Maps to .chartScrollableAxes(_:).

properties.name: visibleDomainLength
properties.notes: .chartXVisibleDomain(length:) number of units visible at once. Range 5...100.

properties.name: annotationOverflow
properties.notes: AnnotationOverflowResolution strategy so the scrolling annotation stays in bounds.

components.title: SurfacePlot (3D surface)
components.summary: 3D-only chart content that renders a mathematical surface y = f(x, z); the sole mark type exclusive to Chart3D.
components.summary_long: Renders a continuous surface from a closure (x, z) -> y inside a Chart3D. Supports multiple surfaces in one chart, explicit x/y/z scale domains, and surface-specific foreground styles (heightBased gradient, normalBased shading).
components.higNotes: New in OS 26 and Chart3D-only. Use heightBased to make surface shape readable via a y-value gradient; normalBased shades by facing direction. Set explicit chartXScale/chartYScale/chartZScale domains to frame the surface. Not available on tvOS or watchOS.

properties.name: surfaceStyle
properties.notes: Maps to .foregroundStyle(.heightBased) / .normalBased / a solid color. heightBased gradients by y-value; normalBased shades by surface normal.

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Solid fill used when surfaceStyle is solid.

properties.name: xDomainLength
properties.notes: .chartXScale(domain: -x...x). Range 1...10. Frames the x extent of the surface.

properties.name: zDomainLength
properties.notes: .chartZScale(domain: -z...z). Range 1...10. Frames the z extent of the surface.

components.title: Chart Overlay & Background (ChartProxy)
components.summary: Overlays or backs the plot area with custom SwiftUI content, using ChartProxy to convert between data values and view coordinates for custom interactivity.
components.summary_long: The foundational customization/interaction surface predating chartXSelection. .chartOverlay { proxy in ... } and .chartBackground { proxy in ... } give a ChartProxy plus a GeometryProxy so you can hit-test gestures, draw custom highlights, and map data<->position via proxy.value(atX:) / proxy.position(forX:). Used for custom tooltips, crosshairs, and lollipop selection on iOS 16 where chartXSelection (17+) is unavailable.
components.higNotes: Use chartOverlay for custom touch interaction and annotations layered above marks; chartBackground for static decoration behind them. Convert gesture locations with ChartProxy and the supplied GeometryProxy. Keep overlay content lightweight; prefer the higher-level chartXSelection on iOS 17+ for simple scrubbing.

properties.name: layer
properties.notes: Switches between .chartOverlay (above marks) and .chartBackground (behind marks).

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: alignment: parameter positioning the custom content within the plot area.

properties.name: showCrosshair
properties.notes: Demo toggle: draw a crosshair/rule at the gesture location using proxy.value(atX:) -> proxy.position(forX:).

properties.name: accentColor
properties.notes: Color of the custom overlay marker / crosshair.
